summary: Small decompilation-matching PR replacing several placeholder functions with matched C across game-mode camera/scene code and SysDolphin baselib particle, TEV, SIS, and SObj helpers. The changes are mostly direct wrapper/setup functions: scene Init iteration in gm_801A3EF4, SIS camera setup in gmCamera_801A2650, render-state setup in hsd_80391A04 and psSetupTevCommon, archive load/free wrappers in sislib, and SObj allocator initialization.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801A3EF4 by iterating the MajorScene table returned by gm_801A50AC until scene->idx == 0x2D and calling each non-NULL scene->Init.

Implemented gmCamera_801A2650 to set gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.x20 to 2, register/load SIS data with HSD_SisLib_803A62A0(3, "SdVsCam", "SIS_VsCameraData"), and store the HSD_SisLib_803A611C result in gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.x54.
Added extern declarations in gmcamera.c for HSD_SisLib_803A62A0 and HSD_SisLib_803A611C, and moved the local gmcamera.h include before platform/placeholder includes.

Implemented hsd_80391A04 in particle.c, including static scale/line-width globals lbl_804D6070, lbl_804D6074, and lbl_804D6078, then setting GX/HSD vertex descriptors, matrices, line width, point size, render mode, and cull mode.
Added baselib/cobj.h and baselib/state.h includes to particle.c to support camera matrix and state setup calls.
Implemented psSetupTevCommon by configuring TEV order, color op, alpha op, and swap mode for TEV stages 0, 1, and 2.
Added dolphin/gx.h include to psdisptev.c for GX TEV APIs.
Implemented HSD_SisLib_803A945C as a thin wrapper around lbArchive_LoadArchive.
Implemented HSD_SisLib_803A947C as a thin wrapper around lbArchive_80016EFC.
Added melee/lb/lbarchive.h include to sislib.c for archive wrapper declarations.
Added global HSD_ObjAllocData HSD_SObjLib_804D10E0 and implemented HSD_SObjLib_803A44A4 to initialize it with HSD_ObjAllocInit(&HSD_SObjLib_804D10E0, 0x9C, 4).
smart_moves: Matched several small isolated functions in one PR, reducing placeholder count without requiring broad architectural changes.
Used direct wrapper functions for archive load/free in sislib rather than over-complicating functions that appear to simply delegate to lbArchive APIs.
Preserved unknown/generated-style symbol names such as hsd_80391A04 and HSD_SisLib_803A945C where semantic names were not established, avoiding speculative renaming.
For gm_801A3EF4, represented the original control flow as a simple table walk with a sentinel scene idx value and a NULL Init guard.
For psSetupTevCommon, kept the repeated GX TEV setup calls explicit, which is often the safest route for matching order-sensitive GX state code.
Introduced only the headers needed for newly matched calls, making dependencies explicit in particle.c, psdisptev.c, and sislib.c.
decomp_lessons: Small render setup functions can often be matched by preserving the exact GX/HSD call sequence and literal arguments rather than abstracting repeated stages into loops.
When a function is just an asset/archive adapter, a thin wrapper around an existing library call may be the correct decompilation result.
Scene-table functions may use sentinel indices rather than explicit counts; matching code should reflect the table-walk condition observed in assembly.
Unknown global state used by rendering helpers may need to remain as address-derived lbl_ names until its broader role is identified.
For allocator setup functions, the object size and alignment/count arguments to HSD_ObjAllocInit are matching-critical literals.
Header inclusion can be part of a clean match: add the specific baselib, GX, or lbarchive headers needed rather than relying on undeclared externs where declarations already exist.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names: gm_801A3EF4, gmCamera_801A2650, hsd_80391A04, psSetupTevCommon, HSD_SisLib_803A945C, HSD_SisLib_803A947C, HSD_SObjLib_803A44A4.
Unidentified static/global data retained lbl/address-style names: lbl_804D6070, lbl_804D6074, lbl_804D6078, HSD_SObjLib_804D10E0.
Existing struct/member naming remained partially inferred or generated, including SisLibUnkStruct2, gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.x20, and gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.x54.
Asset/string identifiers were preserved exactly as literals: "SdVsCam" and "SIS_VsCameraData".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use sentinel-controlled pointer iteration for MajorScene arrays: start from gm_801A50AC(), advance scene++, stop at idx 0x2D, and call Init only if non-NULL.
Keep GX state setup calls in source order for functions like hsd_80391A04 and psSetupTevCommon because register/state sequencing affects matching.
Represent render-state constants as raw literals where no named enum mapping was introduced, such as HSD_SetupRenderMode(0x68000002), TEV argument zeros/fours, and HSD_StateSetLineWidth(..., 5).
Use static file-scope variables for persistent render parameters when the assembly references fixed data addresses.
Thin wrappers can match archive helper functions exactly when they only return or forward an existing call result.
Allocator init matching may require declaring the global allocation data object in the same translation unit and passing exact size/alignment constants.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human PR body text was present in the provided PR slice beyond the title.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbl_804D6070, lbl_804D6074, lbl_804D6078, or HSD_SObjLib_804D10E0.
Search for subsequent work on DrawASCII or hsd_80391AC8 in particle.c to understand adjacent render helper conventions.
Search for follow-up declarations or header moves for HSD_SisLib_803A62A0, HSD_SisLib_803A611C, HSD_SisLib_803A945C, and HSD_SisLib_803A947C.
Look for later gmcamera.c matches around gmCamera_801A26C0 and other SIS camera data setup functions.
Search for other psdisptev.c matches to confirm whether repeated TEV stage setup remains explicit or is later abstracted.
